macro_segments.title: Orlah and Kilayim in Eretz Yisrael versus Chutz L'Aretz: Evidentiary Approach to Status Distinction

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Sugya: The Mishna on Doubts in Orlah and Kilayim and the Distinction Between Them
Ulla's Challenge: If Orlah is Halacha L'Moshe M'Sinai, How Can We Be Lenient in Cases of Doubt?
The Mishna's Detailed Leniences in Chutz L'Aretz: Yored V'Lokeach for Orlah and Kilayim
Shmuel's Response: Emending the Mishna to Have Uniform Leniencies for Both Orlah and Kilayim
Mar Brei D'Ravina's Alternative Reading: Both Orlah and Kilayim Allow Seeing the Picking
Shivi's Proposal: Maintaining Doubt Rather Than Witnessing the Act
Rav Avya and Rabba Bar Bar Chana's Practical Solution: Exchanging Picking Rights
The Sharp Ones of Pumbedita and the Extreme Position: No Orlah in Chutz L'Aretz

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Response to Over-Leniency: The Slippery Slope Problem and the Tradition of Rabbi Eliezer

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda Reports the Leniency to Rabbi Yochanan; Rabbi Yochanan's Severe Countermeasures
The Tradition of Rabbi Eliezer: Ein Orlah B'Chutz L'Aretz as an Unbroken Chain of Authorities
The Apparent Contradiction: Rabbi Eliezer's View on Chadash Versus Ein Orlah B'Chutz L'Aretz
Rav Assi's Teaching in the Name of Rabbi Yochanan: Orlah in Chutz L'Aretz as Halacha L'Moshe M'Sinai
Rav Zeira's Objection: How Can It Be Halacha L'Moshe M'Sinai If the Mishna Teaches Such Leniencies?
Rav Assi's Recovery: The Halacha L'Moshe M'Sinai Itself Incorporates Doubt (Safek Mutar, Vadai Assur)

macro_segments.title: Kilayim in Chutz L'Aretz: Distinguishing Kilayim of the Vineyard from Kilayim of Seeds

micro_segments.title: Rav Yochanan's Teaching: Getting Lashes for Kilayim Even Outside Eretz Yisrael
The Resolution: Harkavat Ilan (Tree Grafting) Applies Outside Eretz Yisrael; Kilayim of Seeds Does Not
Biblical Source and Hermeneutic Reading: Behemtecha Lo Tarbia, Sadecha Lo Tizra
Rav Chanan and Rav Anan Encounter: Planting Seeds in Chutz L'Aretz and the Complexity of the Prohibition
Rabbi Yoshiya's Definition: Kilayim of the Vineyard Only When All Three Seeds Are Planted Simultaneously
Tosafot's Analysis: Distinguishing Kilayim of Seeds (Garden) from Kilayim of the Vineyard (Must Include Grape)
Rav Yosef's Practice in Chutz L'Aretz: Mixing Seeds Together Without Violating Kilayim Prohibitions
Abye's Distinction: Kilayim of the Vineyard vs. Kilayim of Seeds—Only the Former Applies Outside Eretz Yisrael
Summary Chart of Restrictions: What Is Assur B'Hana'ah, What Requires Action, Where It Applies
Practical Implications: Hybrid Fruits (Nectarines, Tangelos) Are Permitted Because Kilayim of Seeds Is Not Assur B'Hana'ah

macro_segments.title: The Reward and Punishment of Mitzvos in This World Versus the World to Come

micro_segments.title: The Mishna on Doing One Mitzvah: Good Is Extended to You and Your Days Are Lengthened
The Contradiction with the Mishna in Peah: Which Mitzvos Yield Fruit in This World?
Rav Yehuda's Resolution: The Mitzvah Must Tip a Fifty-Fifty Scale of Merit and Demerit
The Baraita on Opposite Outcomes: Excess Merit Yields Suffering; Excess Sin Yields Blessing
Abye's Resolution: These Opposite Outcomes Occur on Different Days, Not Over One's Entire Life
Rabbi Yaakov's View: All Reward for Mitzvos Is Exclusively in the World to Come
The Problem of the Righteous Who Suffer: How Can the Torah's Promises of Long Life Be Fulfilled?
Rabbi Yaakov's Answer: All Blessings Refer to the World to Come, Which Is Entirely Good and Eternal
The Gemara's Examination: Can We Use Hypothetical Cases to Challenge Biblical Promises?

Reward in this world (Sechar B'Olam Hazeh)
